quick_facts.label: Conducting body and purpose
quick_facts.value: Xavier Aptitude Test (XAT) is a national-level entrance examination conducted by XLRI Jamshedpur on behalf of XAMI members for admission to MBA/PGDM programmes.

quick_facts.label: Current cycle dates
quick_facts.value: For XAT 2027, registration opened on July 15, 2026; the stated closing date is December 6, 2026; the admit card is listed as available from December 20, 2026 (tentative); and the exam is scheduled for January 3, 2027, from 2:00 PM to 5:00 PM. Dates remain subject to change by the examination authority.

quick_facts.label: Basic eligibility
quick_facts.value: The XAT 2027 FAQ states that applicants need a recognised bachelor's degree of at least three years' duration, or an equivalent qualification, in any discipline. Candidates whose final examinations are completed by June 12, 2027 may apply.

quick_facts.label: Exam mode and duration
quick_facts.value: XAT 2027 is scheduled as a three-hour examination. The official FAQ says there are overall time limits rather than sectional time limits, and candidates can choose and switch between sections during the test.

quick_facts.label: Sections and scoring context
quick_facts.value: The published XAT 2027 overview lists Verbal Ability and Logical Reasoning, Decision Making, Quantitative Aptitude and Data Interpretation, and General Knowledge. It indicates approximately 95 questions, +1 for a correct answer, −0.25 for an incorrect answer, and a −0.10 penalty for each unattempted question after eight; GK has no negative marking and does not count toward the overall percentile/cut-off score.

quick_facts.label: 2027 registration fee
quick_facts.value: For the 2027 cycle, the official registration page lists a XAT registration fee of ₹2,300, with ₹200 extra for each selected XLRI programme. Fees are cycle-specific and may change.

sections.heading: What XAT is for
paragraphs: XAT is an MBA/PGDM entrance examination conducted by XLRI Jamshedpur on behalf of XAMI members. The official XAT site describes it as being used for MBA/PGDM admissions and states that it is accepted by more than 250 B-schools; applicants should nevertheless confirm programme-specific acceptance, eligibility, cut-offs, and selection stages directly with each institute.

A BBE graduate normally fits the examination's discipline-neutral academic route: the published XAT 2027 eligibility criterion is a recognised bachelor's degree of at least three years or equivalent, rather than a specified undergraduate major. Meeting the exam eligibility requirement does not itself create admission eligibility at every accepting school or programme.
bullets: XAT is an entrance-test route, not a management degree or a job credential.
XLRI and other institutes may use the score differently and may add their own application, shortlisting, interview, group-discussion, academic-profile, or work-experience requirements.

sections.heading: Eligibility for XAT 2027
paragraphs: According to the official XAT 2027 FAQ, a candidate needs a recognised bachelor's degree of at least three years' duration, or equivalent, in any discipline. Candidates who complete their final examinations by June 12, 2027 are also eligible to apply. Thus, a BBE holder or eligible final-year BBE student can use this route, subject to the stated completion condition.

For the XAT route described in the FAQ, Indian candidates appear for XAT; NRI, Foreign, OCI, and PIO candidates may apply through GMAT. This is exam-level guidance, so applicants should separately read the admissions policy of every programme to which they intend to apply.
bullets: No BBE-specific subject prerequisite is stated in the published XAT eligibility criterion.
Programme-level rules can be narrower than the examination's general eligibility rule.

sections.heading: Test format and what it assesses
paragraphs: The official XAT 2027 overview presents four areas: Verbal Ability and Logical Reasoning (VA&LR), Decision Making (DM), Quantitative Aptitude and Data Interpretation (QA&DI), and General Knowledge (GK). The listed approximate distribution is 26 VA&LR questions, 21 DM questions, 28 QA&DI questions, and 20 GK questions. VA&LR includes reading comprehension, critical and analytical reasoning, vocabulary, grammar, para-jumbles, and related exercises; DM uses personal and professional scenarios; QA&DI covers foundational quantitative topics and data interpretation; and the GK split shown is eight static-GK and 12 current-affairs questions.

For XAT 2027, the authority lists a three-hour, 2:00 PM–5:00 PM session with no sectional time limits; candidates may begin with and switch among sections. The official pattern also lists one mark per question, a 0.25-mark deduction for wrong answers, and a 0.10-mark deduction for each unattempted question after the first eight. GK has no negative marking and is not included in the overall percentile/cut-off calculation, though XLRI says it may consider GK in its selection process.
bullets: Use the 95-question distribution as an approximate published pattern, not a guarantee of an unchanged future paper.
An on-screen scientific calculator is stated to be available in the test interface.

sections.heading: Applying for the 2027 cycle
paragraphs: As published for XAT 2027, registration opened on July 15, 2026 and is scheduled to close on December 6, 2026. The test is scheduled for January 3, 2027, and admit-card download is listed from December 20, 2026 on a tentative basis. Applicants should treat all timetable information as cycle-specific and re-check the official portal before acting.

The official process directs candidates to complete the online form, enter academic and work-experience details, select XLRI programmes if desired, select up to six preferred test cities, upload a photograph and signature, preview the form, and submit payment. The site cautions that applicant details cannot be edited after submission following payment; names should match certificates, mark sheets, and identity documents.

The published 2027 XAT registration fee is ₹2,300, with ₹200 additional for each XLRI programme selected. This is a dated fee for the 2027 cycle and can change; other XLRI/GMAT routes have separately listed fees.
bullets: Keep the generated application form and payment receipt after a successful transaction.
Choosing XAT does not automatically complete every school or programme's admissions requirements; check each target institute's official admissions page.

sections.heading: Preparation approach grounded in the published pattern
paragraphs: Build preparation around the four published areas rather than treating XAT as only a quantitative or verbal test. In particular, reserve dedicated practice time for Decision Making: the official description says these questions use personal and professional scenarios and require critical thinking, problem-solving, and prioritisation. For QA&DI, practise arithmetic, algebra, geometry, mensuration, modern mathematics, and interpreting and analysing data; for VA&LR, practise reading comprehension, reasoning, vocabulary, grammar, and paragraph-ordering-style tasks.

Use full-length timed practice under the current three-hour overall limit. Because XAT states that candidates may move among sections and that unanswered-question penalties begin after eight skips, review attempts as well as accuracy: choose a section order, test whether switching helps, and practise a deliberate strategy for difficult questions. This is a preparation inference from the official format, not an official prescribed strategy.
bullets: Practise Decision Making separately; its scenario-based format is a distinctive XAT component.
Use the on-screen-calculator feature in practice where possible, but do not rely on it instead of quantitative fluency.
Recheck the final information bulletin and instructions before the examination because patterns and operational rules can be revised.

sections.heading: What a XAT score can lead to
paragraphs: A XAT result can be used to seek admission to MBA/PGDM programmes at XLRI and other accepting business schools. The official XAT site says the exam is accepted by more than 250 B-schools, but acceptance alone should not be read as an offer of admission or as a common cut-off policy.

At XLRI, the published admissions overview states that candidates may apply to multiple programmes and that selection criteria can vary by programme. It says shortlisting and final selection can consider XAT performance alongside factors such as academic background, relevant work experience, extracurricular activities, and personal interviews and/or group discussions. Applicants should therefore prepare for the post-score admission stages and verify the specific programme's current selection rules.
bullets: A strong score is one component of an admissions application, not a guaranteed seat.
Outcomes are educational admissions opportunities; salary, placement, and career results depend on the particular programme, student profile, labour market, and later recruitment process.
